app.title: Chemistry Utility
app.intro: This Chemistry Utility is a free online chemistry toolkit offering molar mass calculation, chemical equation balancing, stoichiometry solving, gas law calculations, dilution formulas, and more.
calculators.elementLookup.title: Element Information Lookup - Atomic Number, Mass, Electron Configuration and more
calculators.elementLookup.description: Enter the element symbol or name of the element you want to look up:
calculators.elementLookup.toolDescriptio: Quickly look up any chemical element to find its atomic number, mass, electron configuration, and group on the periodic table. This tool is perfect for students, teachers, or hobbyists who need fast reference information. Just type the element name or symbol and get accurate, reliable data instantly.
calculators.elementLookup.label: Element symbol or name
calculators.elementLookup.molarMassLinkT: To compute masses using this elemental data, try the
calculators.elementLookup.name: Name
calculators.mass.title: Molar Mass Calculator - Calculate Molecule/Compound Molar Mass Instantly
calculators.mass.toolDescription: This tool helps you quickly find the molar mass of any compound by adding up the atomic weights of its elements. It is useful when preparing solutions, converting grams to moles, and more. Just enter a chemical formula, and the calculator handles all the isotope-averaging math for you.
calculators.mass.label: Chemical formula
calculators.mass.stoichiometryLinkText: After finding molar mass, you can also compute reaction yields using the
calculators.balancing.title: Chemical Equation Balancer - Able to Balance most Chemical Equations
calculators.balancing.toolDescription: Balancing chemical equations can be surprisingly tricky, especially with combustion or multi-step reactions. This tool checks the atom counts on each side and finds the correct coefficients automatically. It is a simple way to verify your work or understand how mass is conserved in a reaction.
calculators.balancing.label: Chemical equation
calculators.balancing.stoichiometryLinkT: Once balanced, you can analyze reactants and yields with the
calculators.dilution.title: Molarity and Dilution Calculator - Prepare Solutions Easily
calculators.dilution.toolDescription: The dilution tool helps you figure out how much stock solution and solvent you need to reach a specific concentration. It uses the classic C1V1 = C2V2 relationship, making it easy to scale solutions up or down. Perfect for preparing lab samples or adjusting buffer strengths.
calculators.dilution.stoichiometryLinkTe: If you're preparing solutions for reactions, check the
calculators.massPercent.title: Mass Percent and Concentration Calculator - ppm (parts per million) and ppb (parts per billion)
calculators.massPercent.toolDescription: This calculator shows the mass percent, ppm, or ppb of a solute in a solution. It is useful for environmental chemistry, purity checks, and analytical experiments. Just enter the amounts, and it quickly provides an easy-to-read breakdown of concentrations.
calculators.mixing.title: Solution Mixing Calculator - Calculate Final Molarity and more
calculators.mixing.toolDescription: Mix two solutions of different concentrations and volumes to find the resulting molarity. This tool is great for preparing reactant mixtures or adjusting stock solutions in the lab. Enter your known values, and it calculates the final solution automatically.
calculators.nuclear.title: Nuclear Chemistry Calculators
calculators.nuclear.halfLifeTitle: Half-Life Calculator - Exponential Decay in Nuclear Chemistry
calculators.nuclear.toolDescription: The half-life calculator models exponential decay to show how much of a substance remains over time. It is helpful in radiochemistry, nuclear physics, and pharmacokinetics. Enter the initial quantity and half-life, and the calculator handles the rest with precise results.
calculators.gasLaws.title: Gas Laws
calculators.gasLaws.toolDescription: Use this suite of gas calculators to solve for pressure, volume, temperature, or moles using the Ideal or Combined Gas Laws. For real gases, the Van der Waals correction adjusts for molecular interactions, giving more accurate predictions. It is perfect for review and more.
calculators.gasLaws.molarMassLinkText: Working with gases? You may also need molar masses from the
calculators.gasLaws.idealTitle: Ideal Gas Law Calculator - PV = nRT
calculators.gasLaws.idealTitleApp: Ideal Gas Law Calculator - PV = nRT
calculators.gasLaws.vdwTitle: Van der Waals Equation - Real Gas Pressure Calculator
calculators.gasLaws.vdwDescription: Calculate Pressure (P) for a real gas.
calculators.electrochemistry.title: Electrochemistry
calculators.electrochemistry.toolDescrip: Calculate standard cell potentials, non-standard conditions with the Nernst equation, and electrolysis mass/current/time relationships. This tool is valuable for redox chemistry, battery experiments, and electroplating calculations. Enter your values, and get instant results for your reactions.
calculators.electrochemistry.cellPotenti: Cell Potential Calculator - Standard and Non-Standard
calculators.electrochemistry.reductionPo: Enter the standard reduction potentials for the two half-reactions:
calculators.electrochemistry.nernstTitle: Nernst Equation Solver - Non-Standard Cell Potential
calculators.electrochemistry.nernstDescr: Calculate cell potential under non-standard conditions.
calculators.stoichiometry.title: Stoichiometry Calculator - Moles, Mass, and Limiting Reactants
calculators.stoichiometry.toolDescriptio: This tool walks you through stoichiometric conversions — moles, mass, molecules, and limiting reactants. It is handy for lab planning or problem-solving because it shows how reactants and products relate through mole ratios. Enter known quantities, and the calculator fills in the rest.
calculators.bondType.title: Bond Type Predictor - Ionic, Covalent, or Metallic
calculators.bondType.description: Enter two element abbreviations to predict the bond type (Ionic, Covalent, or Metallic):
calculators.bondType.descriptionApp: Enter two element abbreviations to predict the bond type:
calculators.bondType.toolDescription: Predict whether a bond is ionic, covalent, or metallic based on electronegativity differences and periodic table trends. This tool is ideal for students learning about chemical bonding. Simply enter the elements, and get a clear prediction with a brief explanation.
calculators.bondType.notPossible: Bond prediction not possible due to unavailable electronegativity data
errors.elementNotFound: One or both elements not found in periodic table
errors.volumeTooSmall: Volume is too small for the given amount of gas (V must be greater than n*b)
errors.firstConcentrationPositive: First solution concentration must be positive
errors.secondConcentrationPositive: Second solution concentration must be positive
errors.firstVolumePositive: First solution volume must be positive
errors.secondVolumePositive: Second solution volume must be positive
errors.enterValidPotentials: Please enter valid numbers for both potentials.
errors.enterValidPositiveNumbers: Please enter valid positive numbers for all fields.
errors.enterValidPositiveItzm: Please enter valid positive numbers for I, t, z, and M.
errors.enterValidPositiveMtzM: Please enter valid positive numbers for m, t, z, and M.
errors.enterValidPositiveMizM: Please enter valid positive numbers for m, I, z, and M.
